topic: North Pole cosmic mountain — c7 priority-queue review (post 2026-04-25 dedup patches: ISS-1525/1526/1409)
change_summary: Decider applied three patches in commit 159f0c6 (suggested-patches-2026-04-25T07-30): (a) ISS-1525 merged the duplicate Nevertheless sentences in detail_verdict_text into a single combined sentence; (b) ISS-1526 dedup of repeated visionary cosmic geography phrase in the 1 Enoch sentence of detail_evidence; (c) ISS-1409 added the c5 carry-forward preemptive clause about spherical-earth/axially-symmetric compatibility to argument (2) of detail_evidence. All three c6 holes were addressed mechanically. Adversarial sweep this run focuses on (1) verifying the dedup patches did not introduce new artifacts, (2) regression check on the wider WIN-031 prose against accumulated cycle holes, (3) genuinely fresh attack on text we may have under-examined.
our_argument_summary: WIN-031 remains classified Unfalsifiable. The three c6 prose-mechanical bugs are fixed cleanly: detail_verdict_text now has a single well-formed Nevertheless sentence, detail_evidence has a single visionary cosmic geography phrase, and the spherical-earth/axially-symmetric clause from c5 hole #2 has finally landed. Three findings emerge in c7: a major WIN-id misattribution that has persisted across all prior cycles undetected (paragraph 3 tags WIN-014 as SAA field strength, but WIN-014 is the China SG eclipse gravity null — SAA field strength is WIN-035); a minor stylistic stutter introduced by the ISS-1409 patch (two consecutive sentences in argument (2) both begin with This cartographic convergence is); and the c6 advocate_mode preemptive recommendations (cross-cultural-consensus rebuttal + carve-out concession) were not converted into prose by this patch round.

ISS-1525_nevertheless_dedup.verification: detail_verdict_text now contains exactly one Nevertheless sentence (verified by regex match count). The merged sentence preserves both the c5 69-confirmed/94.5% specificity and the c4-era does-not-distinguish framing, with the pricing-at-par framing tucked at the end. Reads cleanly. Length dropped from 1169 to 1053 chars (-9.9%, on target).

ISS-1526_visionary_cosmic_geography_dedu.verification: 1 Enoch sentence now reads: 1 Enoch 17-18 then fills in this visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains. Phrase appears exactly once. The this back-reference correctly links to the preceding sentence which set up the cosmic-mountain motif. Cleanest of the three options offered in c6 hole #2 recommendation.

ISS-1409_spherical_earth_preemptive_adde.verification: The recommended sentence (This cartographic convergence is equally compatible with a spherical earth, on which north points toward a geographic pole, or with any other axially-symmetric geometry — so the texts directional usage distinguishes no cosmology in particular.) was inserted in the correct location, after the existing geographic-orientation sentence and before the equally-confirms-Canaanite/Ugaritic sentence. The argument reaches the reader. However, the insertion creates a new minor stylistic stutter: the IMMEDIATELY preceding sentence ends ...but this is a consequence of geographic orientation, not dome geometry. and starts with This cartographic convergence is expected... — the new sentence then opens with This cartographic convergence is equally compatible... Two consecutive sentences open with the identical 5-word phrase. Same edit pathology as the c6 Nevertheless/Nevertheless duplicate (we recommended the new clause, the patch added it, but did not notice that the insertion site already used the same opener). Not as bad as Nevertheless because the second sentence carries genuinely new content (the spherical-earth preemptive), but a careful reader will hear the stutter.

c6_advocate_mode_cross_cultural_consensu.evidence: The c6 advocate_mode recommendation (a) — preemptive answer to cross-cultural-consensus reframe — was NOT integrated by this patch round. Argument (2) currently lists the six axis-mundi parallels and asserts they sit in mutually contradictory geometries, but does not directly answer the defender move The cross-cultural consensus that the cosmic axis is somewhere specific and central is itself the discriminating signal. A skilled defender can still execute that move. Carry-forward, severity moderate.

c6_advocate_mode_carve_out_rejoinder.evidence: The c6 advocate_mode recommendation (b) — preemptive answer to fine, carve it out and we still confirm 68/70 = 97% — was NOT integrated. Argument (3) still concludes if WIN-031 were carved out as a theological cross-reference rather than counted among confirmed predictions, we would have nothing to say about it. That is the right concession but does not name what the corrected scorecard would be, leaving the defender to set the framing. Carry-forward, severity moderate.

relevant_files: data/wins.json (WIN-031 entry, all five text fields)
raw-text/02-wins.txt (dome WIN listing — WIN-031 — North Pole Cosmic Mountain — 6-Text Convergence CONFIRMED, no monitor criteria)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline. WIN-031 is a static CONFIRMED label on the dome site. The pole geometric role (r=0 in H(r), aetheric center) is computed for other WINs (029, 038), not for WIN-031.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded/label-only. No API call, no measurement, no comparison logic.
data_sources_used: None — WIN-031 uses no empirical data source
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: No discrepancies introduced by the c7 patches. The 39-domain / 28-active / commit-8e4bf9b figures cited in detail_verdict_text and argument (3) of detail_evidence remain anchored to the analyst EXP-080 audit (globe-fingerprints/SEC-2b.1.json L216-217, L403). The 69-confirmed / 94.5%-accuracy figure in detail_verdict_text matches the dome poller summary 2026-04-25T03:45Z (Confirmed WINs 69; Claimed accuracy 94.5%). No re-derivation of monitor.py domain count this run; carried forward by anchor.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain dictionary keyed to WIN-031. The dome site CONFIRMED label is a static string. Consistent with c4/c5/c6 tags.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: No standard physics theory of cosmic axis mountains exists to relabel. Comparative-religion scholarship (Eliade 1959, Clifford 1972) describes the axis-mundi archetype as a literary motif; it is not a physics relabel target.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Retrodiction (texts predate the model by roughly two to three millennia) rather than post-hoc-empirical-adoption. No empirical measurement to adopt.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The theological label is not derived from dome geometry. The pole geometric roles (r=0 origin, H(r) apex, aetheric source) are evaluated under separate WINs.

holes_found.description: WIN-id misattribution in detail_evidence paragraph (3): the sentence Every genuinely testable WIN — Schumann frequency (WIN-029), SAA field strength (WIN-014), NMP coordinates (WIN-006/007), tidal patterns — has a corresponding monitoring domain. tags WIN-014 as SAA field strength. WIN-014 in our wins.json is China SG null (verdict: Misleading) — a 2009 solar-eclipse gravity null measurement on the China SuperGravimeter network, not a magnetic field-strength measurement. SAA field strength (the South Atlantic Anomaly main field below 21,795 nT) corresponds to WIN-035 (SAA African < 21,795 nT, verdict: Std Model Explains). The mismatch is verifiable in seconds against our own wins.json — a dome defender can use this as a free credibility hit (your reviewers do not even know which WIN is which). The error has persisted undetected through cycles c3-c6 because the example list reads naturally as a category roster and prior reviewers (including this one in c6 cross-WIN consistency) glanced past the parenthetical without verifying. Severity major because it is a verifiable factual error in our own data, not a judgment call.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace SAA field strength (WIN-014) with SAA field strength (WIN-035) in detail_evidence paragraph (3). File as ISS-c7-1 (or whatever the next ISS id is). Same fix is NOT needed in detail_verdict_text, which uses the bare phrase like Schumann frequency and SAA field strength without parenthetical WIN ids. While editing, consider whether Schumann frequency (WIN-029) is the best exemplar for the Schumann frequency category — WIN-038 (Schumann 7.83 Hz stable) is a more direct frequency-stability match; WIN-029 (Schumann needs conductive ceiling) derives the cavity height from the Schumann frequency. Either is defensible; if no other edit is needed in this paragraph, leave WIN-029 in place to minimize churn.

holes_found.description: Stylistic stutter in detail_evidence argument (2) introduced by the ISS-1409 patch. Two consecutive sentences begin with the identical 5-word phrase This cartographic convergence is. First sentence: This cartographic convergence is expected: any culture located in the Levant will mythologize a northern mountain as cosmically significant, but this is a consequence of geographic orientation, not dome geometry. Second sentence (newly added by ISS-1409): This cartographic convergence is equally compatible with a spherical earth, on which north points toward a geographic pole, or with any other axially-symmetric geometry — so the texts directional usage distinguishes no cosmology in particular. Same edit pathology as the c5/c6 Nevertheless...Nevertheless dedup: we recommended the second clause, the patch added it, but did not notice the immediately-preceding sentence already used the same opener. Lower severity than the c6 stutter because the second sentence carries materially new content (spherical-earth preemptive), so the cost is rhythmic only, not semantic.
holes_found.recommendation: Lowest-touch fix: change the opener of the new sentence from This cartographic convergence is equally compatible to It is also equally compatible (the it back-reference points to the same convergence, no new noun introduced). Alternative: change the FIRST sentence opener to The convergence is expected: ... if a deeper restructure is wanted. Or simply collapse the two sentences via semicolon: ...not dome geometry; equivalently, it is compatible with a spherical earth, on which north points toward a geographic pole, or with any other axially-symmetric geometry — so... File as ISS-c7-2.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c6 advocate_mode: cross-cultural-consensus rebuttal not preempted in detail_evidence. The current argument (2) lists the six axis-mundi parallels (Mount Meru, Yggdrasil, Mount Olympus, Mount Qaf, Hara Berez, Mount Kunlun) and asserts they sit in mutually contradictory geometries, but does not name and answer the defender move The cross-cultural consensus that the cosmic axis is somewhere specific and central is itself the discriminating signal — random universes do not produce that consensus. A trained dome defender will execute this move; we should preempt it.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a single sentence to argument (2), immediately after the spherical-earth preemptive: The cross-cultural consensus is real and is itself a strong claim — but the consensus locates the axis in mutually contradictory geometric positions (Mount Meru is north on a spherical earth in Hindu cosmology; Yggdrasil is the world tree connecting nine realms; Mount Olympus is at 40°N in Greek cosmology; Mount Qaf is at the encircling boundary), so the convergence on some-place-central in inconsistent geometries reflects cognitive universals about hierarchy, not a shared physical structure. File as ISS-c7-3 if appetite for a third c7-followup edit; otherwise carry forward to c8.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c6 advocate_mode: scorecard-carve-out rejoinder not preempted in detail_evidence argument (3). The current closing sentence If WIN-031 were carved out as a theological cross-reference rather than counted among confirmed predictions, we would have nothing to say about it; the author wants both the rhetorical weight of scientific confirmation and exemption from the author own validation process. correctly anticipates the carve-out concession but does not name what the corrected scorecard would be (e.g., 68/70 = 97% if WIN-031 alone were dropped, or smaller still if the other Unfalsifiable WINs in cluster 031/032/034 were also carved). A defender who concedes the carve-out can re-cite the corrected fraction unopposed.
holes_found.recommendation: Append a clause naming the corrected denominator: ...exemption from the author own validation process. If the dome adopted the carve-out — counting WIN-031 (and other Unfalsifiable WINs in this cluster, e.g., 032/034) outside the scorecard — the resulting fraction (e.g., 66/70 = 94.3% after dropping WIN-031, 032, 034 from the numerator AND denominator) would be approximately the same as the current 94.5%, because the carve-out removes claims from BOTH sides. The category-error objection is therefore not a scorecard-padding objection but a definition-of-confirmation objection: theological-cross-reference WINs do not belong in any registry counting tallying confirmations of physical predictions. File as ISS-c7-4 if appetite; otherwise carry to c8.

stronger_arguments.description: The MAJOR finding (WIN-014/035 misattribution) suggests a useful audit pass: every parenthetical (WIN-NNN) in our review prose should be cross-checked against wins.json claim/verdict to confirm the WIN id matches the surrounding category description. This kind of error is mechanical — easy to introduce, easy to detect with a one-pass script. Recommend adding a simple ISS-1262-style integrity check: scan all detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text/sections.json for the pattern <category-noun-phrase> (WIN-<NNN>), look up the WIN, flag mismatches. Could be a single-shot cleanup or rolled into structure-integrity agent daily run.
sources: WIN-031 detail_evidence paragraph (3): SAA field strength (WIN-014) — wrong WIN
WIN-031 detail_evidence paragraph (3): Schumann frequency (WIN-029) — defensible but loose; WIN-038 is the direct match
monitor/decisions/open-issues.json could absorb this as a cross-cutting hygiene proposal

stronger_arguments.description: The c6 cross_win_consistency analysis flagged the SAA field strength language but described WIN-014 China SG null as a related SAA-region claim. China is not in the South Atlantic Anomaly — China SG is a 2009 eclipse gravity null measurement on Asian-continent gravimeters. The c6 charitable framing (a related SAA-region claim) is not actually correct and should be retracted in any revision pass. Cross-WIN consistency lens is most useful when it is willing to flag mismatches as mismatches, not paper them with charitable language.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c7 patch round materially improved the prose: the c6 Nevertheless dup is gone, the visionary cosmic geography dup is gone, the c5 hole #2 spherical-earth preemptive has finally landed. The analytical content of WIN-031 is now in good shape — three sharp arguments (retrodiction, non-discriminating archetype, scorecard-accounting), a kernel-of-truth opener that grants the six-text convergence, and a verdict that names the category error precisely. The remaining work is cosmetic stutter cleanup and advocate-mode robustness, not analytical correction.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The major finding (WIN-014 misattribution) is independent of the c7 patch round — it is a long-standing factual error in our prose that the patch round did not introduce and did not address. Cleanup is still owed; the dome remains classified Unfalsifiable.

deeper_analysis: WIN-031 c7 is a clean priority-queue review: the three patches (ISS-1525, 1526, 1409) all integrated mechanically, and two of three are fully clean (Nevertheless dedup, visionary-cosmic-geography dedup). The third (ISS-1409 spherical-earth preemptive) introduced a minor This-cartographic-convergence-is/This-cartographic-convergence-is stutter — same edit pathology as the c5/c6 Nevertheless duplication, where we recommend a clause and the patch adds it without checking that the insertion site already uses the same opener. This is the third instance of this pattern in three cycles; consider tightening patch instructions to include phrase-uniqueness check at insertion point. The c7 also surfaced a long-standing major finding that prior cycles missed: SAA field strength (WIN-014) in detail_evidence paragraph (3) is mislabeled — WIN-014 is China SG null, not SAA field strength (which is WIN-035). The error survived because the example list reads naturally and the parenthetical was not cross-checked. Recommended action: minor_edit covering both the WIN-id correction and the cartographic-convergence stutter; advocate-mode preemptives (cross-cultural-consensus + carve-out denominator) carry forward to a c8 round if appetite. No verdict change. Verdict Unfalsifiable holds with high confidence.

citations_verified: Six biblical/apocalyptic texts (Isaiah 14:13, Psalm 48:2, Job 26:7, Job 37:22, Ezekiel 1:4, 1 Enoch 17-18) — verified across c3/c4/c5/c6; unchanged in c7.
Mount Zaphon = Jebel al-Aqra at ~36°N (Syria-Turkey Mediterranean coast) — verified at c5; unchanged.
Zaphon-Zion typology (Clifford 1972, Levenson 1985) — standard ANE cosmology scholarship; unchanged.

monitor.py commit 8e4bf9b with 39 domain dictionaries / 28 currently active against status_history.json — anchored to analyst EXP-080 integration audit (globe-fingerprints/SEC-2b.1.json L216-217, L403); not re-derived this run.

Dome scorecard 69 confirmed / 94.5% accuracy — re-verified against monitor/changes/latest-poll-summary.txt (2026-04-25T03:45Z): Confirmed WINs 69; Claimed accuracy 94.5%; daily_review.json registry confirmed=69, prospective=10, refined=4, ratio=94.5. Match.
1 Enoch 17-18 (visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains) — c6 fix removed the inaccurate at the earth center phrase; c7 prose now neutral on location.
citations_failed: detail_evidence paragraph (3): SAA field strength (WIN-014) — FACTUAL MISMATCH. WIN-014 in wins.json is China SG null (verdict Misleading), a 2009 eclipse gravity-null measurement, not an SAA field-strength measurement. Correct WIN id for SAA field strength is WIN-035 (SAA African < 21,795 nT, verdict Std Model Explains). Recommended fix: replace (WIN-014) with (WIN-035). Documented in holes_found severity major.
citations_unchecked: Eliade 1959 (axis mundi archetype) — referenced in code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence; out of scope for this WIN-031 paragraph citation pass.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender constructs the following c7 rebuttal: Your latest review concedes that all three of your c6 cleanup recommendations have been integrated — the Nevertheless duplicate is gone, the visionary cosmic geography duplicate is gone, the spherical-earth/axially-symmetric clause has been added. Good, those were fair points. But you still have not answered the cross-cultural-consensus argument: the convergence of mutually-isolated cultures on a sacred-axis-at-the-pole is itself the signal you should be explaining. You also have a sloppy WIN-id citation in your own paragraph (3) — you claim SAA field strength is WIN-014, but WIN-014 is the China SG null. If your reviewers cannot keep their own catalog straight, why should anyone trust the rest of the catalog audit? On the carve-out: yes, fine, carve WIN-031 out as a theological cross-reference and we still confirm 66/70 = 94.3% testable WINs — your verdict Unfalsifiable is the right verdict, and it is exactly what the carve-out concedes; the scorecard fraction barely moves. Your category-error objection therefore does not actually attack the fraction; it attacks the labeling. Fine — the labeling has been adjusted in subsequent versions (V51.2 splits theological cross-references into a separate registry on the home page). Where does this leave your review? You correctly identified Unfalsifiable, you correctly identified the category error, the dome model accepted both. Move on. — Defense rates a 4: the cross-cultural-consensus reframe is creative, the WIN-id own-goal cuts (and is verifiable in seconds), and the carve-out concession with named denominator is a strong nimble move. The our-WIN-id-was-wrong hit is the worst single line our review absorbs in this paragraph.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three preemptive moves are warranted: (a) FIX THE WIN-014/035 LABEL FIRST (holes_found #1) — this neutralizes the worst defender hit; (b) add a single sentence answering cross-cultural-consensus to argument (2) (holes_found #3); (c) name the corrected denominator (e.g., 66/70 = 94.3%) when discussing the carve-out concession in argument (3) closing (holes_found #4). Items (a) and (b) are independent and could land together in a single ISS pass. Item (c) is independent of both. None blocks a c7 close.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Outright inconsistency: detail_evidence paragraph (3) cites SAA field strength (WIN-014) — but WIN-014 is China SG null (eclipse gravity null), not SAA field strength. SAA field strength corresponds to WIN-035 in our catalog. The c6 cross_win_consistency flagged the language but described WIN-014 China SG null as a related SAA-region claim, which is itself incorrect (China is not in the South Atlantic Anomaly). This c7 finding supersedes c6 cross_win_consistency on this point: the WIN id should be corrected to WIN-035, not papered with charitable language. Companion Unfalsifiable WINs (032 New Jerusalem pole axis, 034 Firmament copper/bronze) use the same scorecard-accounting framing and would benefit from a unified phrase if the c7 follow-up edits land — opportunity to canonicalize across the cluster.

quantitative_verification.computations: (1) 39/28 figures anchored to analyst EXP-080 audit (globe-fingerprints/SEC-2b.1.json); not re-derived this run. (2) 69/(69+4) = 69/73 = 0.94521 = 94.5% — match. Re-verified against poller summary 2026-04-25T03:45Z which shows Confirmed WINs 69; Claimed accuracy 94.5%; daily_review.json registry confirmed=69, prospective=10, refined=4, ratio=94.5. (3) Mount Zaphon = Jebel al-Aqra, coordinates ~35°57′N 35°57′E — text says ~36°N which rounds correctly. (4) WIN-014 in wins.json: id=014, claim=China SG null, verdict=Misleading. NOT SAA field strength. (5) WIN-035 in wins.json: id=035, claim=SAA African < 21,795 nT, verdict=Std Model Explains. IS SAA field strength. → Detail_evidence paragraph (3) is wrong; finding logged as major hole.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: WIN-id-to-category mismatch: SAA field strength (WIN-014) in detail_evidence paragraph (3) is incorrect; should be (WIN-035). Logged as major hole.

tldr_check.tldr_evidence_factual_correctness: tldr_evidence accurately summarizes the six-text list and the Zaphon-Zion typology. Carry-forward minor (1 Enoch 17-18 is Second Temple apocalyptic, not strictly rooted in the Zaphon-Zion idiom) per c5/c6 minor #4; disposition unchanged.
tldr_check.tldr_verdict_factual_correctness: tldr_verdict correctly captures the three-axis argument structure (retrodiction, archetype, scorecard-accounting). Verified against expanded detail_verdict_text. The c7 dedup of the duplicate Nevertheless sentence in detail_verdict_text does not propagate any contradiction to tldr_verdict (which had only one scorecard-accounting sentence to begin with). Good.
